title: Swap One Car Trip for Walking/Biking
description: Replace one short car trip (under 5km) with a walk or bike ride.

title: Use Public Transit for One Commute
description: Leave the car at home and take the bus or train for one of your commutes to work this week.

title: Plan an Eco-Friendly Route
description: Use a mapping app to find a route with less traffic for one of your trips, which can improve fuel efficiency.

title: Check Your Tire Pressure
description: Properly inflated tires improve your vehicle's fuel efficiency. Check your tires and inflate them to the recommended PSI.

title: Avoid Aggressive Driving for a Day
description: Practice smooth acceleration and braking for a full day of driving to conserve fuel.

title: Go Meatless for One Day
description: Choose one day this week to eat completely vegetarian. Avoid beef, chicken, and fish.
source_reference: Based on average daily meat consumption

title: Swap Beef for Chicken in One Meal
description: If you're having a meal with beef, try using chicken instead. It has a much lower carbon footprint.

title: Try a Plant-Based Milk Alternative
description: If you use dairy milk, try switching to oat, soy, or almond milk for your coffee or cereal this week.

title: Finish Your Leftovers
description: Make a conscious effort to eat all your leftovers this week to prevent food waste, a major source of methane emissions.

title: Buy One Local Produce Item
description: Prioritize buying one fruit or vegetable item that is grown locally to reduce 'food miles'.

title: Lower Your Thermostat by 1°C
description: Reduce your home's thermostat by one degree Celsius for the week.

title: Unplug 'Vampire' Electronics
description: Unplug electronics that you're not using, like phone chargers and game consoles, from one power strip.

title: Wash Clothes in Cold Water
description: Do one load of laundry in cold water instead of hot. Most of the energy comes from heating the water.

title: Air Dry One Load of Laundry
description: Instead of using a machine dryer, air dry one load of laundry on a rack or clothesline.

title: Take a Shorter Shower
description: Try to cut your shower time by two minutes for one shower this week. This saves water and the energy to heat it.

title: Skip One Fast Fashion Purchase
description: Resist the urge to buy a new piece of clothing you don't truly need this week.

title: Bring a Reusable Bag
description: Remember to bring your reusable bags for one grocery shopping trip this week.

title: Use a Reusable Coffee Cup
description: If you buy coffee, bring your own reusable cup for one purchase to avoid a disposable one.

title: Learn About Your Local Recycling
description: Spend 15 minutes researching what is and isn't recyclable in your municipality to reduce waste contamination.

title: Cancel One Paper Subscription
description: Switch one paper bill or magazine subscription to digital-only to save paper and transport emissions.
